schedules.name: Daily Status Report
schedules.inlinePrompt: Create a file called status-{date}.md with a brief summary of the project status.

schedules.name: Index-Serving PR Slack scan
schedules.promptTemplate:
Use the Slack tools to scan recent Slack messages available to me across my workspace from the start of today in my local timezone up to now.

Read relevant messages and thread replies. Find every GitHub pull request mentioned in those messages that requires my attention to review. A PR requires my attention if the message or thread tags me directly, mentions my name, asks me to review, asks for my approval, or otherwise implies I am the intended reviewer.

My Slack display name or user ID is: <YOUR_SLACK_DISPLAY_NAME_OR_USER_ID>.

Keep only PRs that belong to the index-serving repository.

For each matching PR, list:
- PR title
- PR number
- URL
- channel name
- author, if available
- Slack message permalink or enough context to find the message
- why it appears to require my review
- any notable review/request/status mentioned in the Slack thread

Only include PRs from the index-serving repo. Ignore PRs from other repos.

Output the result as a concise Markdown table. If there are no matching PRs, say: "No index-serving PRs requiring my review were found in recent Slack messages."

Do not modify source code or repo files.

metadata.description: Disabled smoke-test entry for the index-serving PR review workflow.
